exemplar.title: How and why does discharge increase along the long profile of River Kall?

detailedExemplarData.title: How and why does discharge increase along the long profile of River Kall?

subject.title: Geography
subject.description:
**IB Geography** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **physical and human geography, environmental interactions, global challenges, and spatial patterns**, with a strong focus on **fieldwork, data analysis, case studies, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS)**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Geographic themes - structured questions | 2h 15min | 2h 15min | SL: 35% / HL: 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Geographic perspectives - data response and essays | 1h 15min | 1h 15min | SL: 40% / HL: 45% |
| **Paper 3** | Global interactions - extended essay responses | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Fieldwork investigation (2,500 words) | 20 hours | 20 hours | SL: 25% / HL: 20% |

files.fileName: GEO IA_ How and why does discharge increase along the long profile of River Kall_.pdf

strengths: Research question clearly links to the Bradshaw Model and allows for hypothesis formation.
Precise location mapping with scale bars and place names ensures feasible primary data collection.
High-quality maps include legends, north arrows and scale bars that situate sampling sites unambiguously.
weaknesses: Map 1 is missing a north arrow and scale bar, which are essential for spatial clarity.
The hypothesis could specify expected rates of change or gradients to deepen the theoretical link.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands (question formulation, geographic context, mapping) meet the top-band descriptors with clear theoretical links and precise spatial representation. Minor mapping convention omissions prevent a flawless execution, but overall performance justifies the maximum 3 marks.
feedback.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

strengths: Methods (float timing, depth profiling, width measurement, discharge formula) are described with clear relevance to the research question.
Statistical tests (IQR, RÂ²) are outlined and data collection at 19 sites demonstrates sound implementation.
weaknesses: Method description omits rationale for sampling frequency, risk assessment and specific equipment details.
Typographical errors in formulae (e.g., âsun squared regressionâ) and an inverted IQR formula reduce clarity and accuracy.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student describes and implements appropriate methods that yield reliable primary data, aligning with the mid-band. However, the explanations lack comprehensive justification and contain errors that preclude the top band, warranting 2 marks.

strengths: Extensive dataset of width, depth, velocity and discharge values provides a sound basis for analysis.
Appropriate presentation techniquesâtables, scatter plots, box-plot and line graphsâare used and clearly labelled.
Most conventions (titles, units, axis labels, legends) are followed, aiding interpretation.
weaknesses: Data gaps, outliers and occasional duplicate readings compromise sufficiency.
Mis-typed equations, inconsistent unit formatting and missing axis units in a graph indicate occasional convention errors.
Rationale for outlier removal and criteria for excluding data points is not fully explained.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data are largely relevant and sufficient for partial analysis, and presentation techniques are appropriate. Occasional errors in data sufficiency and conventions place the work in the mid-band, meriting 3 marks.
feedback.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

strengths: Integration of descriptive and statistical techniques (IQR, coefficient of determination) demonstrates solid analytical skill.
Interpretations effectively link frictional concepts and local depositional factors to observed RÂ² values.
Anomalies are identified and contextualized with environmental and methodological explanations.
weaknesses: No confidence intervals or significance tests are reported, limiting depth of analysis.
Incomplete longitudinal profiling and lack of integrated discussion for all variables leave gaps.
Some explanations remain speculative without explicit theoretical linkage.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis goes beyond basic description and employs statistical techniques appropriately, placing it firmly in the 5â6 band. Missing elements such as significance testing and a fully integrated discussion prevent the top band, so 5 marks is justified.

strengths: Conclusion clearly restates findings and cites the strongest RÂ² value in direct reference to the Bradshaw model.
It directly answers the research question and is fully supported by the preceding analysis.
weaknesses: The final thought is incomplete, lacking reflection on how methodological adjustments could refine future work.
Missing a closing sentence to tie together implications for further investigation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion meets the top-band criteria by being clear, focused and fully supported by analysis. Minor incompleteness does not detract from its overall coherence, so it receives the full 2 marks.

strengths: Evaluation outlines relevant methodological strengths and environmental impacts linked to data anomalies.
Suggested improvements (sampling spacing, timing, bias reduction) are feasible and have some explanation of their expected impact.
weaknesses: The impact of suggested improvements on data reliability or RÂ² values is not quantified.
Depth of explanation on how each improvement would alter outcomes is limited.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides a relevant outline of strengths, weaknesses and feasible improvements, aligning with the mid-band descriptors. The lack of quantified impact and depth prevents top-band performance, resulting in 2 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: Two clearly constructed maps include legends, north arrows and scale, satisfying mapping conventions.  The first regional-scale map situates the catchment within Western Europe; the second large-scale map pin-points sampling sites along the channel.  Together they give an unambiguous spatial framework, matching the top band descriptor.

The research question is concise, clearly geographical, and explicitly grounded in the Bradshaw model, permitting the formulation of a directional hypothesis about downstream discharge.  The student demonstrates a solid grasp of syllabus theory by linking variables such as width, depth and velocity to discharge, showing that the enquiry can be tested empirically.  These elements fulfil the descriptor for the highest band.

Tables, scatter plots, box-plot and a line graph are appropriate and clearly labelled.  These techniques allow trends to be discerned but do not exploit more effective options such as log-log scaling or flow-distance diagrams; thus the work sits comfortably in the 3â4 band, allocated 3 marks.

Techniques were implemented correctly (19 usable sites, repeated velocity readings across three transect points) and generated a reasonable volume of primary data.  However, some readings are missing or duplicated, and the use of a single surface float without velocity-area corrections limits reliability, so quality does not reach the âhigh-qualityâ standard.

Beyond basic description, the student applies IQR outlier tests and coefficient of determination to multiple variables, representing an appropriate blend of descriptive and statistical approaches.  No confidence limits or significance testing are attempted, so analysis is not fully comprehensive; nevertheless the work exceeds the 3â4 band and earns 5 within the 5â6 band.

Trends and several anomalies are identified and linked to rainfall, vegetation and methodological haste, showing sound interpretation.  However, some explanations remain speculative (e.g., confirmation bias) and not every pattern is connected explicitly to theory, fitting the 3â4 band at its upper end.

The River Kall is located precisely, with maps identifying the source, dam, and confluence; scale bars and place-names confirm the feasibility of primary data collection along accessible reaches.  The narrative explains why the site is appropriate for testing the Bradshaw model, so location specificity and contextual relevance are fully met.

Strengths, environmental impacts and methodological weaknesses are outlined with some explanation of how each affected results (e.g., storm timing, forest cover).  The discussion is relevant but not exhaustive, so it fits the 2-mark band.

The dataset is extensive and mostly relevant (width, depth, velocity, discharge) but contains gaps and outliers that required removal.  While sufficient for partial analysis, the inconsistencies (e.g., repeated site numbers and zero values) show that not *all* data are fully fit for purpose, meriting the middle band.

The conclusion restates findings, cites the strongest RÂ² and directly answers the question, referencing the Bradshaw model.  It is clearly drawn from preceding analysis, so it is âfully supportedâ, achieving the top band for Criterion E.

The analysis treats the full dataset but leaves gaps: no longitudinal profile or cumulative discharge graph is provided, and statistical outputs are not tested for significance.  Consequently there are evident though not crippling omissions, aligning with the mid-band.

Methods (float timing, depth profiling, width measurement and discharge formula) are described with sound relevance to the question, and statistical tests (IQR, RÂ²) are outlined.  Nevertheless, explanations are brief and omit rationale for sample frequency, risk assessment or accuracy checks, preventing the comprehensive coverage required for the top band.

Most conventions (titles, units, axis labels, legends) are followed, yet a few errors persist (mis-typed equations, missing units in two tables).  These are âoccasionalâ rather than frequent, justifying placement in the 3â4 bracket with 3 marks.

Suggestions address sampling spacing, confirmation bias and timing, and are relevant and feasible.  The student briefly explains expected impacts (greater variety, reduced bias) but does not detail how each improvement would alter data quality in depth, matching the middle band.

annotations.comment: The outlier removal narrative omits criteria for handling negative fence values and the rationale for excluding specific sites.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: The written analysis provides a general summary but lacks explicit linkage between statistical outputs and the research question.
Interpretation of downstream trends would benefit from reporting confidence intervals or significance tests alongside RÂ² values.
The RÂ² interpretation highlights cross-sectional area well but should compare all variables in one integrated discussion.
The research question clearly links to the Bradshaw Model and allows for hypothesis formation, demonstrating strong alignment with geographical theory.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: Figure 1 effectively illustrates the Bradshaw Model and provides a clear theoretical framework for the investigation.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: Typographical errors in the RÂ² formula text (âsun squared regressionâ) reduce clarity; correct to âsum of squared residuals (SSR)â.
The environmental evaluation explains forest influences clearly and links directly to data anomalies.
Methodological limitations are identified but the impact on specific RÂ² values or data reliability is not quantified.
Add a final sentence in the conclusion that reflects how methodological adjustments could refine future investigations.
Map 1 lacks a north arrow and scale bar, which are essential mapping conventions for spatial clarity.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The methodology description omits justification for sample spacing and risk assessment, limiting its comprehensiveness.
The description of the float method uses generic terms (âorangeâ) without equipment specifications, which may confuse reproducibility.
Equation 1 is correctly presented but the units formatting (âm<sup>3 s<sup>-1</sup>â) is inconsistent with conventions.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: The IQR formula is inverted (IQR = Qâ â Qâ), but it should be IQR = Qâ â Qâ to calculate the correct range.
The lowâfence and highâfence values include negative and unrealistic crossâsectional areas; review calculation of quartile-based fences.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Several duplicate site readings (e.g., sites 3 and 5) persist in the raw data table, compromising data sufficiency and requiring correction.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Graph 4 caption omits axis units for both variables, reducing clarity in graphical conventions.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: The discussion of wetted perimeter correlation effectively links frictional concepts to RÂ² findings, adding depth to analysis.
The weak depthâdischarge relationship is well contextualized with local depositional factors, demonstrating sound interpretation.
Suggested improvements would be stronger if tied to anticipated changes in data quality and explained methodologically.
The conclusion succinctly summarizes key RÂ² findings but the final thought is incomplete, leaving the summary hanging.
The hypothesis is well articulated but could specify expected rate of change or gradient to deepen theoretical link.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The introductionâs definition of discharge is clear and concise, framing the study effectively for readers.
